channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Hanukkah, the Jewish Festival of Lights, begins. Here's the history
item.description: Jews around the world are marking the start of Hanukkah, the Festival of Lights, which celebrates the rededication of the Second Temple in Jerusalem in the second century B.C. after it was defiled by Syrian Greeks.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: Beyoncé's performance highlighted Netflix's NFL debut on Christmas Day
item.description: Beyoncé provided more excitement than either game during Netflix’s NFL debut on Christmas Day. Riding into her halftime appearance on a white horse, the 32-time Grammy winner rocked her hometown Houston crowd with a nearly 13-minute performance on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Azerbaijani airliner with 67 people onboard crashes in Kazakhstan leaving 32 survivors
item.description: An Azerbaijani airliner with 67 people onboard crashed Wednesday near the Kazakhstani city of Aktau, leaving at least 32 survivors, according to officials. More than 30 people may be dead.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Pope calls for nations to overcome divisions in Christmas address as communities celebrate worldwide
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — Pope Francis in his traditional Christmas message on Wednesday urged “all people of all nations” to find courage during this Holy Year “to silence the sounds of arms and overcome divisions” plaguing the world, from the Middle East to Ukraine, Africa to Asia.
item.dc:creator: SILVIA STELLACCI and COLLEEN BARRY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Blake Lively's claims that movie co-star launched smear campaign gets support in publicist's suit
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Blake Lively’s claims that “It Ends With Us” director and co-star Justin Baldoni unleashed a smear campaign against her have gained support from a new lawsuit that Baldoni’s former publicist filed against him.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis kicks off a yearlong Jubilee that will test his stamina and Rome's patience
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — Pope Francis kicked off the 2025 Holy Year on Tuesday, inaugurating a celebration of the Catholic Church that is expected to draw some 32 million pilgrims to Rome in a test of the pope's stamina and the ability of the Eternal City to welcome them.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: US census data shows how Middle Eastern and Hispanic residents differ in racial identity
item.description: The U.S. Census Bureau currently is getting public feedback about how it should tally responses from people into new race and ethnicity groups.
item.dc:creator: Mike Schneider, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Dolly Parton's Imagination Library seeks New York state expansion
item.description: Advocates are asking Gov. Kathy Hochul for money in the next state budget to make New York an official state for the program.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Trump campaign won't pay $49,000 in bills for Grand Rapids rallies
item.description: That is forcing the city to write off those expenses as uncollectable and to reevaluate policies for future campaign visits.
item.dc:creator: Ken Kolker, NewsNation

item.title: Stunning photos show lava erupting from Hawaii's Kilauea volcano
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — One of the world's most active volcanoes spewed lava into the air for a second straight day on Tuesday. The eruption of Kilauea volcano on Hawaii's Big Island has stayed within the mountain's summit caldera inside Hawaii Volcanoes National Park. No homes were threatened.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Biden signs a bill officially making the bald eagle the national bird of the US
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The bald eagle, a symbol of the power and strength of the United States for more than 240 years, earned an overdue honor on Tuesday: It officially became the country's national bird.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Illegal immigrant involved in suspected DUI crash that claimed life of Georgia realtor
item.description: A Georgia realtor is dead following a crash that allegedly involved an illegal immigrant from Honduras, who officials say was also an underage drunk driver.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden's rationale behind Christmas present for death row killers foggy at best: expert
item.description: President Biden's rationale behind his move to commute the sentences of 37 of 40 federal inmates on death row remains unclear, according to crime data expert John Lott.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Heavy travel day off to a rough start after American Airlines briefly grounds all flights
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — American Airlines briefly grounded flights nationwide Tuesday because of a technical problem just as the Christmas travel season kicked into overdrive and winter weather threatened more potential problems for those planning to fly or drive.
item.dc:creator: MATT OTT, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Shoppers pack stores for last-minute Christmas gifts
item.description: Some shoppers are in a race to find Christmas gifts before the big man in red arrives to homes across America.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bass, NewsNation

item.title: Why Netflix is streaming NFL games on Christmas Day
item.description: To make the events even more enthralling, Mariah Carey is set to perform ahead of kick-off while Beyoncé is taking over halftime of the Ravens-Texans game.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: Eye drops recalled nationwide due to fungal contamination
item.description: Check your medicine cabinets: A brand of eye drops sold nationally has been recalled due to the presence of fungus, which may cause vision damage.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Self, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mega Millions: Jackpot now over $1 billion
item.description: The Mega Millions jackpot has only been won three times in 2024, the fewest wins in a single year since the game began.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Bill Clinton discharged after hospitalization for flu
item.description: Former President Clinton was discharged Tuesday morning after being hospitalized a day earlier for flu, according to his spokesperson.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Seafarers move global trade
item.description: BARCELONA, Spain (AP) — On a balmy December evening, four Indian crew members, eager to go explore this Mediterranean city where their container ship docked for a few hours, turned for help to Stella Maris.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL’ORTO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Caitlin Clark honored as AP Female Athlete of the Year following her impact on women's sports
item.description: Caitlin Clark raised the profile of women's basketball to unprecedented levels in both the college ranks and the WNBA, and Tuesday she was named the AP Female Athlete of the Year for her impact on and off the court.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Walmart illegally opened bank accounts for 1 million drivers, lawsuit alleges
item.description: Walmart and fintech company Branch Messenger are being sued by the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau for allegedly forcing more than one million delivery workers to use expensive deposit accounts to access their paychecks.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, ABC 4

item.title: Inside the state and federal charges against the man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare’s CEO
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A triptych of criminal charges paints a searing, sometimes disparate portrait of the man accused of ambushing and killing UnitedHealthcare’s CEO Brian Thompson as the executive arrived at a Manhattan hotel for his company’s annual investor conference.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Adnan Syed's lawyers seek to reduce his prison sentence
item.description: ANNAPOLIS, Md. (AP) — Attorneys for Adnan Syed, whose complex legal case was chronicled in the hit podcast “Serial," are seeking to have his prison sentence reduced as he awaits further court decisions.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ethics releases Matt Gaetz report: 5 takeaways
item.description: The report — which spans 42 pages and includes several more in exhibits — found “substantial evidence” that Gaetz violated House rules, state and federal laws, outlining allegations of prostitution, statutory rape and illicit drug use, among other accusations.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Civil rights icon James Meredith is honored in his Mississippi hometown
item.description: KOSCIUSKO, Miss. (AP) — A new historical marker has been unveiled in the hometown of James Meredith, honoring the Black man who fought white supremacy by integrating the University of Mississippi in 1962.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Chat with Santa or find out if you are on the naughty list with these tech features
item.description: We've moved past simply mailing your wish list to the North Pole. Santa has arrived on ChatGPT and you can talk to him.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, Fox 5

item.title: Nordstrom to be acquired by Nordstrom family and a Mexican retail group for $6.25B
item.description: ntury-old department store Nordstrom has agreed to be acquired and taken private by Nordstrom family members and a Mexican retail group for $6.25 billion, the company said Monday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Biden gives life in prison to 37 of 40 federal death row inmates so Trump can't have them executed
item.description: President Joe Biden announced Monday that he is commuting the sentences of 37 of the 40 people on federal death row, converting their punishments to life imprisonment mere weeks before President-elect Donald Trump, an outspoken proponent of expanding capital punishment, takes office.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and DARLENE SUPERVILLE Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 2 Navy pilots shot down over Red Sea in apparent 'friendly fire' incident: US military
item.description: Two U.S. Navy pilots were shot down Sunday over the Red Sea in an apparent “friendly fire” incident, the U.S military said, marking the most serious incident to threaten troops in over a year of America targeting Yemen's Houthi rebels.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What are 'snow rollers'? Photos of rare phenomenon in Michigan spark curiosity
item.description: “This is the coolest thing I've ever seen,” one viewer wrote, remarking on the dozens of doughnut-shaped snowballs peppered across a field.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WGN 9
